note: Advisory sandbox externalization + parity proof. Emits CHECK_OK/CHECK_REJECTED, never PASS. The module constants remain the source of truth.
title: Externalize the Phase-2 rank factor table (parity, reviewable copy)

subject.note: WEIGHTS + FACTORS serialized verbatim into catalog/builds/WI-5/phase2_rank_factors.json; module constants untouched

what_it_proves: WEIGHTS + FACTORS are transported into phase2_rank_factors.json VERBATIM (deep-equal to the live module constants)
score recomputed from the JSON = round(sum(WEIGHTS[k]*FACTORS[mid][k] for k in WEIGHTS), 2) for every move == the module's score
ranking recomputed from the JSON (sort by -score, move_id) == the ranking from the in-module constants: ['M03','M05','M04','M02','M06','M07','M01','M09']
a MINIMAL MUTATION of one factor (M09 direct_revenue 0.1 -> 1.0) shifts M09 from last to 2nd and its score 50.5 -> 82.0 -> CHECK_REJECTED (the parity check discriminates)
mutating a single WEIGHT also re-rejects (no relaxation admits a drifted table)
verify() computes in-memory only: it never runs the ranker main(), the phase2_queue_v1.json output is never written/touched
running the tool leaves scripts/p0pgr_phase2_rank_v1.py byte-identical (sha unchanged); module constants NOT deleted
chess_patches_applied: data JSON generated by importing the module constants (guarantees verbatim, no hand-transcription drift)
negative fixture is a MINIMAL MUTATION of the real on-disk artifact (one factor value), rejected — no strawman, no relaxation
positive proof is the unmutated real JSON accepted at parity with the live constants
in-memory compute only; ranker main() never invoked, no queue side effect; source module & its constants left intact (Lock 5)
origin=sandbox-advisory / authority=none; reports parity_reasons, never its own governance PASS
